plan_type_rationale: Fix on core pipeline path (orchestrator.ts) with test requirement; strict gates ensure correctness before close.

phases.name: Understand and reproduce the false-stale case
phases.description: Read the two diverging extractors to understand the exact symbol-set difference and identify a concrete reproducer.

tasks.description: Read src/pipeline/orchestrator.ts lines 460-504 (staleness check) and src/semantic/header-generator.ts lines 110-135 (element.exports aggregation)
tasks.success_criteria: Understand exactly which symbol sets each path produces and why they diverge

tasks.description: Read src/pipeline/extractors/relationship-extractor.ts extractRawExports method and src/pipeline/semantic-elements.ts buildSemanticElementsFromState to map both export derivation paths
tasks.success_criteria: Both extraction paths fully understood; divergence documented

tasks.description: Identify a concrete file in src/ that exhibits the false-stale condition (has a valid @exports header but gets headerStatus=stale). Verify by reading its @exports line and comparing to rawExports output.
tasks.success_criteria: At least one concrete false-stale file identified with proof

tasks.description: Commit analysis notes to analysis.json (update with specific diverging symbols)
tasks.success_criteria: analysis.json updated with concrete divergence example

phases.name: Apply fix and add regression test
phases.description: Replace rawExports-based astSet with element.exports-derived set in the staleness check. Add a test that covers both the true-stale and false-stale cases.

tasks.description: In orchestrator.ts lines 473-480: replace `const astSet = new Set(rawExports.map(e => e.exportedName))` with a set derived from the same element.exports source that header-generator uses. The elements array is already available in scope (line 460). Build the astSet from elements.flatMap(e => e.exports?.map(ex => ex.name) || []).
tasks.success_criteria: astSet is derived from element.exports, not rawExports. The `astSet.delete('default')` line can be removed (element.exports never contains 'default').

tasks.description: npm run build — verify TypeScript compiles clean after the edit
Run npm test -- --testPathPattern='output-validation-semantic-headers|orchestrator' to verify existing header-status tests still pass
tasks.success_criteria: All targeted tests pass; no regressions

tasks.description: Add test case in __tests__/pipeline/output-validation-semantic-headers.test.ts: create a fixture file with @exports matching element.exports but containing symbols not in rawExports (e.g. a type-only export). Assert headerStatus=current after the fix.
tasks.success_criteria: New test passes, documents the false-stale case

tasks.description: Run full npm test to verify no regressions across all 1495+ tests
Stage and commit the orchestrator.ts fix and new test

phases.name: Verify end-to-end: --source-headers produces headerStatus=current
phases.description: Run populate-coderef --source-headers on a file that was previously false-stale and confirm headerStatus=current in the re-scan.

tasks.description: Run: node dist/src/cli/populate.js --source-headers --overwrite-headers --select index on the coderef-core project itself (dog-food pass). Capture the headerStatus of the previously-false-stale file.
tasks.success_criteria: The previously false-stale file now shows headerStatus=current in .coderef/index.json

tasks.description: Verify that a genuinely stale file (one whose @exports header does NOT match its actual exports) still shows headerStatus=stale. This confirms the fix didn't mask real staleness.
tasks.success_criteria: A file with a genuinely wrong @exports header shows headerStatus=stale

tasks.description: Final commit: update communication.json with verification results and push

hard_stops.reason: Terminal gate — requires end-to-end verification before close
